title: StructuredActionPlan
description: v1. Output contract for the Reasoning LLM's second inference pass (vision.md section 4/5). This is the ONLY channel between the LLM and the deterministic engines -- every field here must be resolvable by a deterministic engine (temporal reasoning, contact resolution, etc.) without further LLM judgment calls. Grammar-constrained via GBNF derived from this schema (see research/llama-verify).

goal.description: The single top-level action the assistant intends to take. Closed enum -- the Action Validator (vision.md section 3) dispatches on this value, so it can never be free text.

confidence.description: LLM's self-reported confidence that `goal` and `slots` correctly capture the user's intent. Deterministic Validation (section 3) uses a threshold below which it asks for clarification instead of acting -- separate from the retrieval relevance/confidence filter in open question 7.3, which gates context injection rather than final-plan execution.

slots.description: Typed, structured fields extracted from the utterance plus any retrieved memory context. This is where 'NER becomes a schema-constraint problem' (vision.md section 8) -- every reference type gets an explicit typed slot instead of a free-text span.

raw_text.description: Verbatim span, e.g. 'Rahul'. Entity linking (7.2) resolves this to a contact_id downstream -- the LLM never emits a contact_id itself.

fuzzy_time_hint.description: e.g. 'yesterday', 'this Friday' -- resolved by the temporal engine, not computed here.

raw_text.description: e.g. 'the document we discussed' -- routed to vector memory search, never resolved by the LLM directly.
e.g. 'tomorrow at 8:30', 'an hour before'. The LLM extracts the span; it does NOT compute the resolved datetime -- that's the Temporal Reasoning Engine's job (section 2).

numeric_constraints.description: Real utterances routinely carry more than one simultaneously (vision.md section 2's own example has three: arrive_before, travel_duration, wake_before_departure) -- must be an array, not a single slot.

retrieved_context_used.description: Which memory records (if any) the reasoning pass actually relied on. Lets the Action Validator and eval harness (7.8) trace whether a wrong action came from bad retrieval vs. bad reasoning.

clarification_needed.description: Non-null iff the plan cannot be safely executed as-is (e.g. retrieval found nothing, per open question 7.7, or confidence is below threshold). When set, the Action Validator must surface this instead of dispatching `goal`.
